question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the mirror node to accept a minority-hash stream file as consensus by injecting a crafted StreamFileSignature with a manipulated fileHash that matches a small group of colluding nodes whose combined stake just meets the 1/3 threshold, while the legitimate majority hash group's signatures are suppressed or delayed, causing the mirror node to record a reorged transaction history?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that totalStake is read exclusively from the first signature in the collection (signature.getNode().getTotalStake()) to artificially lower the consensus threshold by ensuring a crafted signature with an inflated node.totalStake value appears first in the collection, making a minority file hash appear to reach consensus and causing transaction history reorganization?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger the zero-totalStake fallback path (where all node stakes are 0 and the code substitutes stake=1 per node) by poisoning the NodeStakeRepository with zero-stake entries, then submitting signatures for a minority file hash from a sufficient number of fake nodes to reach the 1/3 node-count threshold, causing the mirror node to accept a reorged transaction history?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the HashMultimap grouping by getFileHashAsHex() to split legitimate node signatures across multiple hash buckets by injecting signatures with slightly different fileHash byte arrays that produce the same hex string (hash collision or encoding ambiguity), preventing any single group from reaching consensus and causing a denial of history ingestion?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a transaction history reorganization by exploiting the fact that consensusCount > 0 is the only check after the loop, meaning if an attacker can get even one hash group to reach consensus with manipulated stake values, the validate() method returns successfully regardless of whether that hash represents the true network-agreed file?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the ordering of the signatures collection to ensure that a node with a disproportionately large totalStake value is processed first, setting stakeRequiredForConsensus to a value derived from that inflated totalStake, then providing signatures from nodes whose combined stake meets this manipulated threshold for a minority file hash, causing history reorg?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user bypass the consensus check entirely by ensuring the signatures collection is non-empty and commonDownloaderProperties.getConsensusRatio() equals BigDecimal.ZERO, exploiting the early-return path at line 54-57, so that any non-empty set of VERIFIED signatures for any file hash causes the mirror node to accept that file without stake-based validation, enabling history reorganization?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the validate() method to accept a fraudulent file hash by injecting signatures whose SignatureStatus is already set to VERIFIED (bypassing the upstream signature verification step) for a minority file hash, then ensuring those signatures' combined node stake meets the 1/3 threshold, causing the mirror node to record a reorged transaction history?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit integer overflow in the stake accumulation loop (stake += signature.getNode().getStake()) by injecting signatures with extremely large stake values (near Long.MAX_VALUE) that overflow to a negative or small positive number, causing canReachConsensus() to incorrectly evaluate a minority hash group as having reached consensus and accepting a reorged history?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getStakeRequiredForConsensus()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a SignatureVerificationException by injecting a signature whose node.getTotalStake() returns 0 as the first element in the collection, causing getStakeRequiredForConsensus(0) to throw, which could be used as a denial-of-service to prevent legitimate stream files from being ingested and delay the mirror node's view of transaction history?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: canReachConsensus()] Can an unprivileged external user manipulate the BigDecimal comparison in canReachConsensus() by crafting a scenario where stake is exactly equal to stakeRequiredForConsensus (boundary condition), and by controlling which node's totalStake is used to compute the threshold, make a minority file hash group appear to reach consensus at the exact boundary, enabling transaction history reorganization?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that stakeRequiredForConsensus is computed using RoundingMode.CEILING to craft a scenario where the ceiling rounding causes the threshold to be set to a value that a minority hash group's stake can meet while the majority group's stake cannot, by manipulating the totalStake value read from the first signature, causing the wrong file hash to be accepted as consensus?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a NullPointerException or unexpected behavior in getFileHashAsHex() by injecting a StreamFileSignature with a null fileHash byte array, causing DomainUtils.bytesToHex(null) to return null, which is then used as a key in the HashMultimap, potentially corrupting the signature grouping logic and allowing a reorged file hash to be accepted?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the validate() method reads totalStake only once from the first signature and never validates that all other signatures in the collection report the same totalStake, allowing an attacker to inject signatures with inconsistent totalStake values that do not affect the threshold calculation but whose individual stakes are counted toward a minority hash group, enabling history reorganization?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the mirror node to accept a minority-hash stream file by exploiting the absence of any cross-validation between the filename stored in the first signature and the filenames of subsequent signatures, injecting signatures for different filenames into the same validate() call, causing the consensus check to be performed against a mixed set of signatures that do not all correspond to the same stream file?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the HashMultimap's non-deterministic iteration order (HashMultimap uses hash-based ordering) to cause the debugStake tracking variable to reflect a different hash group than the one that actually reached consensus, obscuring the true consensus state in logs and making it harder to detect a history reorganization attack?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user cause multiple hash groups to simultaneously reach consensus (consensusCount > 0 for more than one group) by crafting signatures where the same node appears in multiple hash groups with different fileHash values, causing the mirror node to mark signatures from conflicting file hashes as CONSENSUS_REACHED and creating an ambiguous transaction history state?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that validate() does not verify that the node IDs in the signatures collection are unique, allowing duplicate node entries to be injected for the same node with the same stake value, artificially inflating the stake sum for a minority file hash group to meet the consensus threshold and causing transaction history reorganization?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the early-return condition (consensusCount > 0) combined with the HashMultimap iteration to ensure that a crafted minority hash group is evaluated first in the keySet() iteration, causing it to be marked CONSENSUS_REACHED before the legitimate majority hash group is evaluated, and then having the method return early without ever evaluating the legitimate group?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a transaction history reorganization by exploiting the fact that the validate() method sets CONSENSUS_REACHED status on signatures in-place (via setStatus()) without any atomicity guarantee, so that if the method is called concurrently for the same signature collection, race conditions could cause signatures from a minority hash group to be marked CONSENSUS_REACHED while the legitimate group's status update is overwritten?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of validation that node.getStake() is non-negative in the stake accumulation loop, injecting signatures with negative stake values to reduce the computed stake for a legitimate hash group below the consensus threshold while a minority hash group with only positive stakes reaches consensus, causing the mirror node to accept a reorged transaction history?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getStakeRequiredForConsensus()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the BigDecimal multiplication (BigDecimal.valueOf(totalStake).multiply(commonDownloaderProperties.getConsensusRatio())) with a very small consensusRatio value close to zero to produce a stakeRequiredForConsensus of 1 (after CEILING rounding), making it trivially easy for any single node's stake to meet the consensus threshold for a minority file hash, enabling history reorganization?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the validate() interface accepts a generic Collection<StreamFileSignature> without enforcing any ordering contract, injecting a custom Collection implementation whose iterator() returns elements in attacker-controlled order, ensuring that a signature with a manipulated totalStake is always returned first to set a favorable consensus threshold for a minority file hash?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the mirror node to skip consensus validation for a fraudulent stream file by ensuring the signatureHashMap is non-empty and consensusRatio equals BigDecimal.ZERO, exploiting the bypass at line 54, where the check uses BigDecimal.ZERO.equals() (value equality) rather than compareTo(), potentially allowing a consensusRatio of 0.0 with different scale to bypass the check and accept any file hash?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/ConsensusValidator.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: validate()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the validate() method does not verify the cryptographic integrity of the fileHash bytes themselves (only groups by hex representation), injecting signatures where the fileHash is a preimage collision or truncated hash that maps to the same hex string as a legitimate file hash, causing the minority group's signatures to be merged with the legitimate group and inflating its apparent consensus?
